data.title: Aurora Cannabis to invest in German facility upgrades
data.description:
• Aurora Cannabis (NASDAQ:ACB) on Thursday announced an investment of over five years into operational upgrades at its EU-GMP manufacturing facility in Leuna, Germany.
  • These improvements are expected to increase flower growth capacity, product quality and drive cost efficiency.

data.title: European indexes climb after Fed interest rate cut, BOE holds rate steady at 4%
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.09% to 9,223, Bank of England holds interest rate steady at 4% citing subdued growth.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.19% to 23,639, outperforming its European peers.

France (CAC:IND) +1.13% to 7,875. 

The economy of Georgia grew by 7.3% Y/Y in Q2.

Switzerland's trade surplus narrowed to CHF 3.9 billion in August.

The Netherlands' seasonally adjusted unemployment rate rose to 3.9% in August.

The Eurozone’s current account surplus narrowed to EUR 35 billion in July 2025 from EUR 39.3 billion a year earlier. The goods surplus fell to EUR 27.9 billion from EUR 35.3 billion, and the services surplus dropped to EUR 19.9 billion from EUR 20.4 billion. 

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.74% higher to 554.7, with most sectors and all major regional bourses in positive territory as trackers digest the latest after the Federal Reserve’s widely expected 25 bps cut, with policymakers flagging two more reductions this year but just one in 2026.

Meanwhile, the Bank of England held the benchmark interest rate steady at 4% during its September 2025 meeting, with a vote tally of 7–2 to keep the rate unchanged.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 3 basis points to 4.05%.

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.68%.

UK's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 4.63%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Commercial Metals to buy Concrete Pipe & Precast in $675M deal
data.description:
Commercial Metals (NYSE:CMC) said Thursday it agreed to acquire Concrete Pipe & Precast, a supplier of precast concrete solutions to the U.S. Mid-Atlantic and South Atlantic regions, from Eagle Corporation and ECPP LLC for $675M in cash.

Commercial Metals (NYSE:CMC) said the acquisition will broaden its portfolio of early-stage construction solutions with targeted customer groups and bring it onto the construction site earlier in the life of a project, enhancing its ability to participate in construction activity from site preparation to structural erection.

The acquisition establishes a highly scalable platform in a fragmented industry with strong margin characteristics, the company said, as the top 10 precast suppliers comprise less than 25% of the domestic market, with only two national players and a relatively small number of sizable regional producers.

The company expects the acquisition will be immediately accretive to earnings per share and free cash flow per share, as well as annual run-rate synergies of $5M-$10M by the third year, primarily related to optimization initiatives.

data.title: Northern Lights acquires the Pup Copper project in the Yukon
data.description:
• Northern Lights Resources (OTCPK:NLRCF) said on Thursday that it has entered into an option agreement with veteran prospector Bernie Kreft to acquire a 100% interest in the Pup Copper Project, located in the Yukon Territory.
  • The project is located approximately 10 kilometres west of the regional centre of Dawson City. It consists of 64 quartz claims totalling approximately 1,300 hectares.  
  • The project is situated within the mineral rich Yukon Tanana Terrane, host to numerous significant Cu-Au porphyry, VHMS base metal and orogenic gold type targets, deposits, or past producing mines. 
  • The company has the right to acquire a 100% interest in the Pup Project by paying $5,000 in cash, issuing 7.5M shares and completing $1M in exploration over 3 years.

data.title: Eli Lilly posts late-stage trial win for Ozempic in children and adolescents with diabetes
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has announced detailed results from a Phase 3 trial in which its GIP/GLP-1 dual receptor agonist, tirzepatide, marketed as Mounjaro, reached the primary endpoint in children and adolescents with type 2 diabetes.

Citing detailed data from the 99-patient global trial as presented at a European medical event and simultaneously published in the medical journal The Lancet, the Indiana-based drugmaker stated that Mounjaro met the primary and all key secondary endpoints in its SURPASS-PEDS study.

The popular injectable is already available in the U.S. for adults with type 2 diabetes, and marketed as Zepbound for obese adults or some overweight adults with at least one weight-related medical condition.

Based on a per-protocol analysis, the company noted that SURPASS-PEDS met the study’s main goal, as tirzepatide led to a 2.2% average decline in A1C, a key diabetes biomarker, outperforming the placebo, which caused an average A1C reduction of 0.05%.

Safety results were in line with the known tolerability profile of the gut hormone-based therapeutic class known as incretin drugs, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) said, noting that, however, 3% of those in the on-drug arm discontinued the trial due to adverse events compared to none in the placebo arm.

The company added that it has filed SURPASS-PEDS data with multiple regulatory agencies worldwide to expand Mounjaro’s indication.

data.title: Telus International files circular ahead of shareholder vote on proposed deal with Telus Corp
data.description:
Telus International (Cda) (NYSE:TIXT) Thursday said that it has filed its management information circular and accompanying materials in connection with the special meeting of shareholders to consider the proposed arrangement with Telus Corporation (NYSE:TU).

Telus Digital also announced that on September 17, the Supreme Court of British Columbia granted an interim order respecting the arrangement, authorizing the calling and holding of the special meeting.

The circular gives shareholders details on Telus’s plan to buy all remaining shares of Telus Digital for $4.50 each, valuing the deal at $539 million.

The arrangement provides a 52.0% premium over the unaffected price, with multiple consideration options available to shareholders.

data.title: Saudi Arabia inks mutual defense deal with nuclear-armed Pakistan
data.description:
Saudi Arabia and Pakistan have signed a mutual defense agreement, under which any aggression against either country will be considered as an attack on both, following Israel's strike on Qatar last week.

The pact "aims to develop aspects of defense cooperation between the two countries and strengthen joint deterrence against any aggression," according to a joint statement from Saudi Arabia and Pakistan.

To note, Pakistan is the only Muslim-majority country that is nuclear-armed. Pakistan and Saudi Arabia have had a decades-old defense relationship.

A Saudi Arabian official told Reuters that the latest agreement is the culmination of years of discussions, and "not a response to specific countries or specific events."

Pakistan developed its nuclear weapons program as a deterrent against India. Saudi Arabia also maintains close ties with India.

"We will study the implications of this development for our national security as well as for regional and global stability," India's foreign ministry said in a statement.

data.title: Nvidia, Intel sign blockbuster deal to co-develop chips; Nvidia to take $5B stake in Intel
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) announced a blockbuster deal on Thursday to co-develop PC and data center chips. Simultaneously, Nvidia said it would take a $5B stake in Intel.

Nvidia shares rose 2.2% in premarket trading, while Intel soared 18%.

As part of the deal, the two companies will use Nvidia's NVLink, bringing Nvidia's artificial intelligence and accelerated computing strength and Intel's x86 architecture. Intel will build Nvidia-custom x86 CPUs for the data center, and Intel will build and offer x86 system-on-chips that integrate into Nvidia's RTX GPUs. The RTX system-on-chips will be aimed at the PC market.

In addition to the deal, Nvidia said it would invest $5B in Intel's common stock at a price of $23.28 per share. The investment is subject to customary closing conditions, including required regulatory approvals, the companies said.

“AI is powering a new industrial revolution and reinventing every layer of the computing stack — from silicon to systems to software. At the heart of this reinvention is NVIDIA’s CUDA architecture,” said NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang in a statement. “This historic collaboration tightly couples NVIDIA’s AI and accelerated computing stack with Intel’s CPUs and the vast x86 ecosystem — a fusion of two world-class platforms. Together, we will expand our ecosystems and lay the foundation for the next era of computing.”

“Intel’s x86 architecture has been foundational to modern computing for decades — and we are innovating across our portfolio to enable the workloads of the future,” said Lip-Bu Tan, CEO of Intel. “Intel’s leading data center and client computing platforms, combined with our process technology, manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities, will complement NVIDIA’s AI and accelerated computing leadership to enable new breakthroughs for the industry. We appreciate the confidence Jensen and the NVIDIA team have placed in us with their investment and look forward to the work ahead as we innovate for customers and grow our business.”

The two companies will hold a press conference at 1 p.m. EST to discuss the deal.

This is breaking news...

data.title: Panther Minerals appoints Ram Kumar as CEO and Director
Pentair announces Hydra-Stop acquisition
data.description:
• Pentair (NYSE:PNR) Thursday said that it has completed the previously announced transaction to acquire Hydra-Stop LLC from Madison Industries for approximately $290 million.
  • When adjusted for approximately $50 million of expected tax benefits, the net transaction value is approximately $240 million.
